name: North Carolina Community Housing And Consulting, Inc
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit housing counselor in Fayetteville, NC offering free and low-cost homebuyer education, foreclosure prevention, and rental assistance counseling since 2016.
description_long:
North Carolina Community Housing & Consulting, Inc. (NCCHC) is a nonprofit organization established in 2016 and based in Fayetteville, North Carolina. The organization is HUD-approved, meaning it meets federal standards for housing counseling and operates as a legitimate community development resource. The company's mission centers on bridging the gap between financial literacy and stable housing access for individuals and families in their service area.

NCHC offers a range of housing-focused services including one-on-one counseling ($50), virtual homebuyer education (8 hours, listed as free but shown as $100 for registration), self-paced homebuyer education ($100), foreclosure prevention counseling, predatory lending education, rental housing counseling, and eviction prevention services. Their website emphasizes comprehensive guidance on mortgage qualification, homeownership navigation, tenant rights, and financial planning. They also operate a pre-approval pathway and maintain a resources section with tools and forms for housing-related decisions.

NCHC distinguishes itself through its HUD-approved status, which indicates third-party validation of counselor credentials and service quality. The organization explicitly positions itself as a nonprofit committed to community stability rather than profit extraction. Their service array spans the full housing lifecycle—from first-time buyers to foreclosure prevention to renters facing eviction—demonstrating breadth of housing expertise. The 8-hour homebuyer education courses meet standard pre-purchase requirements for many mortgage programs.

A key limitation is that services appear concentrated in the Fayetteville area (physical office at 321 Dick Street), making access difficult for those outside Cumberland County. Pricing is modest but not entirely free despite nonprofit status—core one-on-one counseling costs $50 and education courses cost $100, which may present barriers for extremely low-income households. The website lacks detail on income eligibility thresholds, funding sources, or outcomes data. No information is provided about rapid-response capacity during housing crises.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling agency—counselors meet federal certification standards
8-hour homebuyer education meets pre-purchase requirements for many mortgage lenders
Foreclosure prevention service directly addresses mortgage default risk
Eviction prevention counseling targets renters facing immediate housing loss
One-on-one counseling available for personalized guidance ($50)
Predatory lending education helps borrowers identify deceptive loan products
Operating as 501(c)(3) nonprofit since 2016 with documented community focus
Virtual homebuyer education option increases accessibility beyond local office
cons: Services appear geographically limited to Fayetteville/Cumberland County area despite statewide name
Education courses ($100) and counseling ($50) fees may create barriers for lowest-income households despite nonprofit status
Website provides no income eligibility criteria, making it unclear who qualifies for services
No published outcome data, success rates, or client testimonials to verify impact
Limited details on response time for urgent services like eviction or foreclosure prevention
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Fayetteville/Cumberland County seeking pre-purchase counseling and education
Homeowners facing mortgage default or foreclosure who need negotiation support
Renters at risk of eviction needing financial counseling and tenant advocacy
Prospective borrowers wanting to understand and avoid predatory lending practices
diagnosis: NCCHC is best for Fayetteville-area residents seeking legitimate, federally-approved housing counseling at modest cost, particularly first-time homebuyers and households facing foreclosure or eviction. The main caveat is geographic limitation to the local area and lack of outcome transparency—consumers should verify service availability for their specific location before contacting.
services: 8-Hour Homebuyer Education - Virtual (free per description, $100 registration)
Lease Review and Tenant Rights Education
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
